torch_pattern: weighted = combine * weights; out = torch.sum(weighted, dim=EXPERT_DIM)
applicability_notes: MoE weighted-combine pattern: elementwise mul by routing weights followed by sum over the expert dimension (dim=0, 1, or EXPERT_DIM depending on layout). Taken from tt_moe.py test ('to_layout(TILE) + mul + sum') and repeated at many MoE call sites (mixtral, gpt-oss, deepseek). Contiguous chain — candidate for fused weighted-reduce.

placement_observations.source: model call site: tt_sum = ttnn.sum(tt_weighted, dim=0, keepdim=True) then typecast bfloat8_b and all_reduce with memory_config=ttnn.L1_MEMORY_CONFIG

placement_observations.size_descriptor: [seq_len, emb_dim] summed over topk_dim before reduce_scatter
placement_observations.source: model call site: TtReduceModule, surrounding weighted-sum path uses output_memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG; unweighted fallback ttnn.sum(combine_output, dim=self.topk_dim)
